topic.name: Tariff Schedule Amendments
topic.description: Documents discussing temporary adjustments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States for specific products.

Document 1: duty-free treatment of acrylic filament tow
imported in the form of 8 sub-bundles crimped together
Document 2: temporary suspension of duty on mixtures or coprecipitates
lanthanum phosphate, cerium-doped lanthanum phosphate, cerium phosphate, and terbium phosphate
Document 3: temporary reduction of duty on certain golf bag bodies

topic.name: Housing and Small Business Support Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses legislation aimed at improving housing affordability and supporting small businesses through financial assistance and adjustments in rental policies.

Document 1: Fair Local Adjustment for Tenant Rents Act of 2014 - Amends the United States Housing Act of 1937 to revise and eliminate specified requirements with respect to public housing agencies establishing flat rental amounts for PHA dwelling units.
Changes $50 from a maximum to a minimum monthly rental amount for individuals residing in public housing.
Document 2: Investing in America's Small Businesses Act of 2016 - This bill amends the Community Development Banking and Financial Institutions Act of 1994 to require the Community Development Financial Institutions Fund to make grants to community development financial institutions.
A community development financial institution must provide non-federal matching funds equal to 50% of the amount of any grant received.

topic.name: Privacy and Security Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at enhancing privacy and security in communication and criminal justice systems.

Document 3: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to prohibit a provider of commercial mobile or commercial mobile data service from providing service on a mobile electronic device reported to such provider as stolen.
Directs providers to make available to account holders the capability of deleting, from a remote location, all information that was placed on such a device after its manufacture.

topic.name: Educational Standards and Improvement
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing educational standards, assessments, and literacy from early childhood through grade 12.

Document 1: Amends the National Assessment of Educational Progress Authorization Act to require science assessments in grades four, eight, and twelve.
Establishes the American Standards Incentive fund to award competitive grants to states for adopting voluntary mathematics and science standards.
Document 2: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act to coordinate school improvement with early childhood education programs.
Requires states to create early learning guidelines and standards for preschool and early elementary education.
Document 3: Authorizes grants to states to improve literacy from birth through grade 12.
Requires national evaluation of the implementation and effect of literacy initiatives supported by this Act.

topic.name: Tax Incentives and Relief Measures
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at providing tax relief and incentives related to family support, health care, and property transactions.

Document 1: allow a surviving spouse to exclude from gross income up to $500,000 of the gain from the sale
the sale or exchange occurs within two years of the death of the spouse
Document 2: increase and expand tax incentives for employer-provided dependent care assistance
establishes tax credits for small employer dependent care assistance program start-up costs
Document 3: exempt from the excise tax on excess benefit employer-sponsored health care plans
contributed to an Archer medical savings plan, a tax-exempt health savings account

topic.name: Marine and Environmental Research Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the research, conservation, and management of marine and environmental resources.

Document 1: establish within NOAA a coordinated national ocean exploration program
increase scientific knowledge essential for the informed management, use, and preservation of oceanic, marine, and coastal areas
Document 2: support a pilot program under which its political subdivisions and housing authorities use grant funds to supplement ongoing bed bug prevention and mitigation activities
Document 3: protection and restoration of the marine waters, habitats, and species of the Northwest Straits region
establish the Northwest Straits Marine Conservation Foundation as a nonprofit foundation to support the Commission

topic.name: Energy Tax Incentives
topic.description: Legislation aimed at promoting alternative energy sources and improving energy efficiency through tax credits and deductions.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code, with respect to the tax credit for alternative fuel vehicle refueling property expenditures
Increases the 30% energy tax credit for investment in fuel cell property
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to adjust the excise tax on liquefied natural gas
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to increase and extend for three years the new energy efficient home tax credit
Increase the rate of the tax deduction for energy efficient commercial buildings

topic.name: Crime Prevention and Safety
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at enhancing public safety through crime prevention, law enforcement collaboration, and community interventions.

Document 1: Amends the Sex Offender Registration and Notification Act to require sex offenders required to register under such Act to notify their residential jurisdictions of their intent to travel from the United States to another country.

Establishes the Child Exploitation Investigations Center within the CCC to coordinate all agency child exploitation initiatives relating to child exploitation, child trafficking, and forced child labor.
Document 2: Amends the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968 to allow funds provided under the Matching Grant Program for School Security to be used for law enforcement and school information sharing activities.
Enabling law enforcement to consult with schools to develop emergency plans, including such plan data, to ensure that plans are comprehensive, complete, and current.
Document 3: Establishes the National Commission on Public Safety Through Crime Prevention to carry out a comprehensive study of the effectiveness of certain crime and delinquency prevention and intervention strategies.

Authorizes the Attorney General and the Director of the National Institute of Justice to make grants and coordinate federal efforts to implement and evaluate innovative crime or delinquency prevention or intervention strategies.

topic.name: Veterans and Military Benefits
topic.description: Legislation related to benefits and honors for military personnel and their families.

Document 1: Gold Star Parents Annuity Act of 2010
special pension to each person who has received a Gold Star lapel button
Document 2: interment or inurnment at Arlington National Cemetery
members of the Army who served honorably for at least nine months in the Tomb Guard Platoon
Document 3: Landing Pass Extension Act of 2015
conditional permit to land temporarily in the United States

topic.name: Legal Rights and Protections
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at ensuring due process and rights for individuals in legal and employment contexts.

Document 1: Authorizes the presiding judge of a U.S. appellate court or U.S. district court to permit the photographing, electronic recording, broadcasting, or televising to the public of court proceedings
Prohibits a presiding judge from permitting the photographing, electronic recording, broadcasting, or televising of any juror in a trial proceeding.
Document 2: Amends the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968 to set forth the due process rights, including procedures, that shall be afforded a law enforcement officer
Establishes rights of officers before and during questioning, including the right to counsel.
Document 3: This bill amends the National Labor Relations Act to require the National Labor Relations Board to certify, without an election, an individual or labor organization to be the exclusive representative of the employees

The preliminary investigation of charges involving employer discrimination or unfair labor practices while employees of that employer were seeking representation by a labor organization shall be made forthwith.

topic.name: Airport Operations and Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the regulation and operation of airports and airline services in the United States.

Document 1: Prohibits the Secretary of Transportation and the Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) from taking actions to regulate the scheduling of airline operations
includes auctioning rights or permission to conduct airline operations at that airport
Document 2: Prescribes a special rule to require the Secretary of Transportation (DOT) for FY2015 and FY2016 to apportion amounts for airport planning and development
based on the number of passenger boardings at the airport during calendar year 2012

topic.name: Land Management and Conservation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the management, conservation, and utilization of public lands, including the establishment of foundations and land conveyances for indigenous tribes.

Document 1: establishes the Bureau of Land Management Foundation as a charitable, nonprofit organization
support the management of BLM lands with regard to wild free-roaming horses and burros, fish and wildlife and their habitats
Document 2: approximately 26,565 acres of land in Nevada administered by the Bureau of Land Management
requires the Tribe to use the land for traditional and customary uses, stewardship conservation for the benefit of the Tribe
Document 3: Ratifies the Salmon Lake Area Land Ownership Consolidation Agreement
Directs the Secretary of the Interior to carry out all actions required by the Agreement

topic.name: U.S. Intelligence and Transparency Legislation
topic.description: This cluster discusses legislative measures related to the U.S. intelligence community and transparency in government information access.

Document 1: require federal agencies to make their disclosable records and documents available for public inspection in an electronic format
prohibit an agency from withholding information requested under FOIA unless the agency reasonably foresees that disclosure would harm an interest protected by a FOIA exemption
Document 2: This bill authorizes FY2017 appropriations for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities of: the Office of the Director of National Intelligence (ODNI); the Central Intelligence Agency (CIA)
the amounts authorized, and the authorized personnel ceilings, are those in the classified Schedule of Authorizations

topic.name: Water Resource Management
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative acts focused on the management and allocation of water resources in different regions of the United States.

Document 1: Klamath Basin Restoration Agreement for the Sustainability of Public and Trust Resources
establishes the Klamath Drought Fund
Document 2: harvest limitations of Pacific cod by the longline catcher processor subsector
protect coastal communities and other Gulf of Alaska participants
Document 3: Regional Water System to divert and distribute water to the Pueblos
establishes the Aamodt Settlement Pueblos Fund

topic.name: Telecommunications Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on amending the Communications Act to enhance access and regulation of telecommunications services.

Document 1: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to extend the exemption from retransmission consent requirements
Requires designated market areas for purposes of determining a satellite carrier's obligations
Document 2: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to require the FCC to require that customer premises equipment provide internal means for effective use with hearing aids

Defines the term 'telecommunications relay services' to mean telephone transmission services that provide the ability for an individual who is deaf, hard of hearing, deaf-blind, or who has a speech disability to engage in communication
Document 3: Amends the Departments of Commerce, Justice, and State, the Judiciary, and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2001 to require the FCC to modify the rules authorizing the operation of low-power FM radio stations
Prohibits the FCC from reducing the minimum co-channel and first- and second-adjacent channel distance separation requirements

topic.name: Regulatory Amendments in Education and Health
topic.description: This cluster discusses legislative amendments related to health and safety regulations in educational and athletic contexts.

Document 1: prohibit the provision of federal education funding to state or local educational agencies
provide unemancipated minors with postcoital emergency contraception
Document 2: suspend temporarily the duty on certain tamper resistant ground fault circuit interrupters
Document 3: authorize appropriations for the U.S. Anti-Doping Agency
preventing the use by U.S. amateur athletes of performance-enhancing genetic modifications

topic.name: Social Security Reforms
topic.description: Legislative changes aimed at improving benefits and eligibility criteria for Social Security programs.

Document 1: amends title II (Old Age, Survivors and Disability Insurance) (OASDI) of the Social Security Act
cost-of-living computation requirements
Document 2: amends title II (Old Age, Survivors and Disability Insurance) of the Social Security Act
prescribe formulae for payment of Social Security Disability Insurance benefits
Document 3: amends title II (Old Age, Survivors and Disability Insurance) of the Social Security Act
repeal the seven-year restriction on eligibility for widow's and widower's insurance benefits based on disability

topic.name: Educational Equity and Access
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at improving access to education and addressing equity gaps in educational resources and opportunities.

Document 1: authorize the Department of Education (ED) to award competitive grants to up to 10 institutions of higher education (IHEs) to conduct pilot programs to reduce the cost of attendance for undergraduate students
preference is given to applications that demonstrate a commitment to serving disadvantaged students.
Document 2: Requires the Secretary of the Interior to make one-year grants of $200,000 each to community groups, organizations, or institutions to carry out projects
through which the community may learn about U.S. historical events in the past century through the oral histories of community members.
Document 3: require state and local educational agencies to conduct an annual equity gap assessment for career and technical education programs of study
create plans on how to reduce the gap.

topic.name: STEM and Career Education
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation and initiatives aimed at enhancing STEM education and career and technical education programs in the United States.

Document 1: STEM Education Coordination Act of 2009
coordinating federal programs and activities in support of STEM education
Document 2: Strengthening Career and Technical Education for the 21st Century Act
reauthorizes the Carl D. Perkins Career and Technical Education Act of 2006
Document 3: Entrepreneurial Development Act of 2009
programs for small businesses administered by the Small Business Administration

topic.name: Energy Resource Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating and expanding the development and management of energy resources such as oil, gas, and geothermal energy.

Document 1: Amends the Sherman Act to declare it illegal for foreign states to limit oil production or distribution
Authorizes the Attorney General to bring an action in U.S. district court to enforce this Act.
Document 2: Amends the Geothermal Steam Act to allow noncompetitive leases for geothermal development
Requires Interior to lease the land at fair market value.
Document 3: Requires the Secretary of the Interior to conduct oil and gas lease sales
Establishes the Deficit Reduction Energy Security Fund.

topic.name: Regulation of Controlled Substances
topic.description: The documents address legislative measures related to the regulation and enforcement of laws concerning controlled substances and consumer safety.

Document 1: Amends the Controlled Substances Act
increasing prison terms for manufacturing, distributing, or possessing certain large amounts of heroin, marihuana, or methamphetamine.
Document 3: Amends the Consumer Product Safety Act
prohibits either the Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) or any state attorney general from initiating an enforcement proceeding under the Consumer Product Safety Act.

topic.name: Legal Reforms and Compensation
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at providing legal remedies and compensation for victims of injustices, including torture, trade embargoes, and immigration issues.

Document 1: Terminates the authority of the President to grant the government of Iraq immunity from actions by victims of terrorism seeking compensation
Defines various adequate settlement amounts, depending on the victim, length of torture or detainment, etc.
Document 2: Amends the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 to repeal the embargo placed upon all trade with Cuba
Permits travel to and from Cuba by U.S. citizens or residents.
Document 3: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to eliminate the time limit on the filing of a motion to reopen a removal proceeding
basis of the motion is fraud, negligence, misrepresentation, or extortion.

topic.name: Higher Education Financial Aid
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at reforming and improving financial aid processes and support for students in higher education.

Document 1: amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965
increases the adjusted gross income threshold used to determine whether a student qualifies for an automatic zero expected family contribution
Document 2: amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965
require institutions of higher education and loan originators to include in the information they are required to provide Direct Loan borrowers
Document 3: amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965
requires institutions of higher education to refund to students the tuition and fees paid for education they will not receive due to their withdrawal from school to serve in the Armed Forces

topic.name: Wildlife and Judicial Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislation related to wildlife conservation and judicial administration, particularly focusing on the management of wildlife refuges and the restructuring of court circuits.

Document 1: Amends the National Wildlife Refuge System Administration Act of 1966 to designate a portion of the Arctic National Wildlife Refuge (ANWR) in Alaska as a component of the National Wilderness Preservation System under the Wilderness Act.
Document 2: Judicial Administration and Improvement Act of 2016
divides the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit into a new Ninth Circuit and a newly established Twelfth Circuit.

topic.name: Environmental Regulation and Emissions Control
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures and amendments related to the regulation of greenhouse gas emissions and the authority of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) under the Clean Air Act.

Document 1: Amends the Clean Air Act to prohibit the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) from promulgating any regulation concerning the emission of a greenhouse gas (GHG) to address climate change.
Exempts from such prohibition implementation and enforcement of the rule, 'Light-Duty Vehicle Greenhouse Gas Emission Standards and Corporate Average Fuel Economy Standards'.
Document 2: Prohibits the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) from finalizing any rule imposing a standard of performance under the Clean Air Act for carbon dioxide emissions from any existing or new source.
Prohibits the Administrator from combining in the same category of stationary sources an electric utility steam generating unit and a combined cycle electric generating unit.
Document 3: Amends the Clean Air Act to add as 'extreme and unusual circumstances' under which the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) may temporarily waive a control or prohibition on the use of a fuel or fuel additive.
Requires EPA to publish a revised list reflecting the reduction in the number of fuels.

topic.name: Immigration Policy and Reform
topic.description: This cluster addresses various legislative measures related to immigration, particularly focusing on the treatment and admission of aliens with military service, criminal charges, and border crossing regulations.

Document 1: expresses the sense of Congress that the Department of Homeland Security should exercise its discretion to admit to the United States an alien who is inadmissible
amended to exempt from deportation an alien who has been a member of the Armed Forces for at least 180 days
Document 2: amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to require the detention of an alien who is deportable for failing to maintain nonimmigrant status
U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement shall make reasonable efforts to obtain information about the identity of any victims of the crimes for which such alien was charged or convicted
Document 3: amends the Immigration and Nationality Act provide that the initial admission period for an alien with a valid machine-readable biometric border crossing identification card
prohibits the admission or admission extension of such an alien if the alien previously violated nonimmigrant status conditions

topic.name: Social Security and Disability Benefits
topic.description: Legislation related to the administration and modification of Social Security benefits and disability compensation.

Document 1: disqualified from receiving Social Security disability benefits
deemed to have rendered services in a month if he or she is entitled to unemployment compensation
Document 2: revise the formula for the computation of minimum Old Age Insurance benefits
additional benefit increases for such beneficiaries
Document 3: occupational illness compensation to the survivors of a Department of Energy contractor employee
Requires payment to a survivor of the compensation the deceased contractor employee would otherwise have been paid

topic.name: Sanctions and Foreign Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at imposing sanctions on countries like North Korea, Iran, and Russia to address issues related to terrorism, human rights abuses, and energy exploitation.

Document 1: North Korea Sanctions and Policy Enhancement Act of 2015
civil and criminal penalties under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act
Document 2: Fight Russian Energy Exploitation (FREE) Act
expedited application and approval process for the importation or exportation of natural gas
Document 3: Iran Terrorism and Human Rights Sanctions Act of 2016
mandatory sanctions with respect to Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps and entities it owns

topic.name: Veterans Benefits Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at improving the benefits and support available to veterans, particularly regarding travel allowances, disability compensation, and mental health services.

Document 1: Prohibits the Secretary of Veterans Affairs from imposing any income or pension eligibility requirement
travel allowance or reimbursement for the transportation of veterans to obtain medical care
Document 2: Defines 'combat with the enemy' for purposes of proof of service-connection for veterans' disability compensation
service on active duty in a theater of combat operations during a period of war
Document 3: Allows the receipt of both military retired pay and veterans' disability compensation
full concurrent receipt of both veterans' disability compensation and military retired pay for service-connected disabilities

topic.name: Mental Health and Care Coordination
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation focused on improving mental health services, care planning for advanced illnesses, and ensuring program integrity within health care systems.

Document 1: Creates in the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) an Assistant Secretary for Mental Health and Substance Use Disorders
establish a program of tele-psychiatry and primary care physician training grants to states
Document 2: covers advanced illness planning and coordination services furnished to an eligible individual with progressive illness
establish a Care Planning Advisory Board
Document 3: change from discretionary to mandatory the authority of the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to suspend Medicare and Medicaid payments pending investigation of credible allegations of fraud
establish an information sharing program regarding beneficiary medical ID theft

topic.name: Government Legislation and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at enhancing government operations and public services, focusing on security, healthcare, and telecommunications.

Document 1: Establishes the United States Commission on an Open Society with Security to study how the government may provide for both security in, and public access to, federal buildings and other federal property and sites.
government may provide for both security in, and public access to, federal buildings
Document 2: Amends the Public Health Service Act to increase the maximum amount of funds provided for the establishment and operation of the Organ Procurement and Transplantation Network
Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to request that the Executive Director of the Network report to Congress on the effects of the heart allocation policy change of 2006.
Document 3: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to repeal provisions relating to assistance for the planning and construction of public telecommunications facilities
regarding grants for such facilities and a requirement to develop a related long term plan.

topic.name: Financial Regulatory Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative acts aimed at regulating financial practices and protecting consumers, employees, and retirees in financial contexts.

Document 1: This bill requires individuals, corporations, or other non-government entities that access, maintain, communicate, or handle sensitive financial account information to implement an information security program
notify consumers, state and federal law enforcement, appropriate administrative agencies, payment card networks, and consumer reporting agencies of certain data breaches of sensitive information
Document 2: Amends federal bankruptcy law governing expenses and claims to increase to $20,000: allowed unsecured claims in the fourth order of priority
Includes among prerequisites for confirmation of a business reorganization bankruptcy plan provision for recovery of damages payable for the rejection of a collective bargaining agreement
Document 3: Amends the National Housing Act to direct the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development to establish and collect additional annual premium payments for the first 11 years of the term of an insured mortgage
Directs the Secretary to establish a process for mortgagees to appeal indemnification determinations

topic.name: Renewable Energy and Efficiency Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative measures aimed at promoting renewable energy sources, enhancing energy efficiency, and addressing climate change impacts through various programs and funding initiatives.

Document 1: Sets forth a schedule for motor vehicle fuel, home heating oil, and boiler fuel to contain specified volumes of renewable fuel by calendar year 2022.
Amends the Energy Policy Act of 2005 to increase funding for bioenergy research and development.
Document 2: Requires the Office to promote energy efficiency efforts for, and efforts to reduce energy costs of, small businesses.
Develop a carbon footprint website that contains information on how small business concerns can reduce their carbon footprint.
Document 3: Establishes the Low Greenhouse Gas Transportation Fund.
Requires states and MPOs to establish goals for reducing greenhouse gas emissions from the transportation sector.

topic.name: Food and Drug Administration Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the regulation and safety of food, drugs, and medical products overseen by the FDA.

Document 1: Ensuring Greater Food Safety Act of 2010
establish a plan to ensure effective information sharing regarding the regulation and inspection of food products
Document 2: Combination Product Regulatory Fairness Act of 2016
amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to revise provisions regarding products that are a combination of a drug, medical device, or biological product
Document 3: Expanding and Promoting Expertise in Rare Treatments Act of 2012

direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services to ensure that opportunities exist for consultation with external experts to promote the efficiency of and inform the review by the Food and Drug Administration

topic.name: Health Care Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative acts aimed at improving health care access, patient privacy, and coverage for specific health services.

Document 1: repeals a provision of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act
imposes an annual fee on a health insurance provider based on its net premium income.
Document 2: amends the Public Health Service Act to permit disclosure of patient records relating to substance abuse
develop and disseminate standards for hospitals and physicians regarding displaying a patient's history of opioid addiction.
Document 3: Amends the Public Health Service Act, the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974
require a group health plan to provide coverage for certain types of cancer screening.

topic.name: Small Business Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at supporting and regulating small businesses through tax incentives and procurement opportunities.

Document 1: Restoring Tax and Regulatory Certainty to Small Businesses Act of 2012
extending temporarily: (1) the 100% exclusion from gross income of gain from the sale or exchange of small business stock
Document 2: Small Business Contracting Revitalization Act of 2010

require each federal agency to include in each solicitation for any contract award above the substantial bundling threshold of that agency a provision soliciting bids by teams and joint ventures of small businesses
Document 3: Protecting Taxpayers in Transportation Asset Transfers Act
establish a program for: (1) the attachment of federal liens to public transportation assets

topic.name: Tariff Suspension Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the temporary suspension of duties on specific goods in the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on certain sawing machines.
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain women's footwear.
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain leathered footwear for women.

topic.name: Public Health Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving public health through education, prevention, and treatment of various health issues.

Document 1: Emergency Contraception Education Act of 2007
develop and disseminate to the public information on emergency contraception
Document 2: Comprehensive Tuberculosis Elimination Act of 2007
develop, enhance, and expand information technologies that support tuberculosis control
Document 3: National Black Clergy for the Elimination of HIV/AIDS Act of 2009
authorize grants to public health agencies and faith-based organizations to conduct HIV/AIDS prevention, testing, and related outreach activities

topic.name: Land Exchange and Management Acts
topic.description: These documents discuss various legislative measures concerning land exchanges, management, and conservation efforts in specific regions of the United States.

Document 1: Extinguishes all claims and all right, title, and interest in specified Indian and non-Indian land as part of the settlement of disputes within the Fort Hall Indian Reservation.
Requires the non-Indian land to be held in trust by the United States for the Tribes.
Document 2: Amends the Lincoln County Land Act of 2000 to require implementation of a multispecies habitat conservation plan.
Requires establishment of cooperative agreements between the Bureau of Land Management and Lincoln County for certain county-provided law enforcement and planning related activities.
Document 3: This bill directs the Department of Agriculture to accept the offer and convey to BHI 83 acres of specified federal land.
The USDA shall determine the values of the lands to be exchanged through appraisals following specified requirements.

topic.name: Campaign Finance Reform
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing transparency and accountability in federal election campaign contributions and expenditures.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to revise the requirement of a 48-hour notification of a campaign contribution of $1,000 or more to the Federal Election Commission (FEC)
Requires Senate candidates to file designations, statements, and reports directly with the FEC
Document 2: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 and the Internal Revenue Code to set forth requirements for the disclosure of independent federal election-related contributions and expenditures
Defines 'independent federal election-related activity expenditure' to mean any expenditure that...

topic.name: Government Regulations and Appropriations
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts related to government regulations, appropriations for federal agencies, and policies affecting education and public safety.

Document 1: Reauthorizes and amends authorities relating to the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration's Commissioned Officer Corps (NOAA Corps).
Directs the Secretary to prescribe obligated service requirements for appointments, training, promotions, separations, continuations, and retirement of officers not otherwise covered by law.
Document 2: Requires each state educational agency and local educational agency (LEA) that receives federal funds to prohibit school personnel, contractors, and resource officers from subjecting students to seclusion, mechanical or chemical restraint.
Requires school personnel imposing physical restraint to be trained and certified by a state-approved crisis intervention training program.
Document 3: Authorizes FY2010-FY2011 appropriations for the Department of State to carry diplomatic and consular affairs.
Authorizes FY2010-FY2011 appropriations for contributions to international organizations.

topic.name: Healthcare Program Innovations
topic.description: Legislative efforts to improve and expand healthcare services through innovative programs and support mechanisms.

Document 1: Amends title XI of the Social Security Act to authorize the Secretary of Health and Human Services to waive applicable general and Medicaid requirements of the Program of All-Inclusive Care for Elderly (PACE)
Prohibits the Secretary from waiving certain requirements regarding enrollment in and disenrollment from PACE programs.
Document 2: Amends title XIX of the Social Security Act to require state Medicaid programs to provide medical assistance for patient navigator services.
Patient navigators are trained individuals who facilitate care and have knowledge of the communities they serve.
Document 3: Amends title XVIII of the Social Security Act to provide for coverage of marriage and family therapist services under Medicare part B
Authorizes marriage and family therapists to develop discharge plans for post-hospital services.

topic.name: Financial Regulation and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing transparency, accountability, and stability within financial institutions and markets.

Document 1: Amends the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 and the Investment Company Act of 1940 to require brokers and dealers of mutual funds to disclose in writing the related commissions they receive
Requires the SEC to study and report to certain congressional committees on designated topics, including the level of financial literacy among clients.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Financial Institutions Examination Council Act of 1978 to require a federal financial institutions regulatory agency to make a final examination report to a financial institution within 60 days
Grants a financial institution the right to appeal a material supervisory determination contained in a final report of examination.
Document 3: Amends the Financial Stability Act of 2010 to require the Financial Stability Oversight Council to consider the appropriateness of imposing such standards as opposed to other forms of regulation
FSOC must study every five years the impact of its determinations to subject nonbank financial companies to Federal Reserve Board supervision.

topic.name: Transportation Safety Regulations
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at enhancing safety protocols and investigation procedures in various transportation sectors.

Document 1: Reauthorizes appropriations to the National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB) for FY2011 and FY2012.
Revises the authority of the NTSB to investigate transportation accidents.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Mine Safety and Health Act of 1977 to require the Secretary of Labor to conduct health and safety related accident investigations.
Requires an independent accident investigation by an independent panel for any accident involving three or more deaths.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security to prescribe regulations that require the use of security cameras at all screening locations.
Prescribes a civil penalty of up to $10,000 for individuals who enter an aircraft or an airport area in violation of security requirements.

topic.name: Economic Development and Assistance
topic.description: Legislation focused on economic development, disaster assistance, and community service programs to enhance regional collaboration and support for affected sectors.

Document 1: Amends the Public Works and Economic Development Act of 1965 to authorize appropriations for economic development assistance programs
encouraging and supporting public-private partnerships that promote innovation and entrepreneurship
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to provide assistance to producers of specialty crops in disaster counties
Directs the Secretary to provide unemployment assistance and temporary mortgage rental assistance to California agricultural workers

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments
topic.description: Legislative changes to the Internal Revenue Code aimed at providing tax benefits and incentives for various sectors including employee ownership, environmental conservation, and middle-class families.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend to all domestic corporations, including S corporations, provisions allowing deferral of tax on gain from the sale of employer securities to an S corporation-sponsored employer stock ownership plan (ESOP)
Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to establish the S Corporation Employee Ownership Assistance Office
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to treat gain or loss from the sale or exchange of a mitigation bank credit by the sponsor of the mitigation bank as the sale or exchange of a capital asset held for more than one year
Exempts from tax withholding requirements any gain from sales of mitigation bank credits
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to increase the child tax credit for the first year in which a child is claimed as a qualifying child
Replaces the Hope and Lifetime Learning tax credits with an expanded education tax credit

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments
topic.description: Legislative changes to the Internal Revenue Code affecting various tax provisions and credits.

Document 1: repeal provisions that provide for the advance payment of the earned income tax credit.
Document 2: reduce from $18 to $9 the per-barrel tax on beer
allow a further reduction in such tax for brewers who produce not more than 2 million barrels annually.
Document 3: increase the amount of the income and excise tax credits for biodiesel used as fuel.

topic.name: Legislative Acts on Agriculture and Housing
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at regulating agricultural disaster assistance, tobacco trafficking, and housing finance reforms.

Document 1: Prevent All Cigarette Trafficking Act of 2009 or PACT Act
revises provisions governing the collection of taxes on, and trafficking in, cigarettes and smokeless tobacco.
Document 2: Heartland, Habitat, Harvest, and Horticulture Act of 2007
establish a program of supplemental agricultural disaster assistance.
Document 3: FHA Modernization Act of 2007
amends the National Housing Act to modify requirements for the maximum principal loan obligation.

topic.name: Legislative Amendments and Authorizations
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures aimed at amending existing laws and extending authorizations for government programs and services.

Document 1: Amends the Fish and Wildlife Act of 1956 to extend through FY2017 the authorization of appropriations for volunteer services
community partnership projects for national wildlife refuges, and refuge education programs.
Document 2: Amends the District of Columbia Home Rule Act to eliminate congressional review of newly-passed District laws.
Document 3: Requires the Postal Service to ensure that every post office has access to running water and bathroom facilities.
Prohibits the Post Office, in making a determination whether or not to close or consolidate a post office, from considering whether the post office meets the requirements of this Act.

topic.name: Social Welfare Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. legislation aimed at improving social welfare through various programs and funding mechanisms.

Document 1: Peace Stamp for the Peace Corps Act directs funding for the Peace Corps.
Issuing a semipostal stamp to support specific causes.
Document 2: Claims presented to the Indian Health Service under the Indian Self-Determination Act.
Timeliness of claims related to health services for Native Americans.
Document 3: America's Right-to-Eat Rescue Act affects eligibility for food assistance programs.
Adjusts poverty line considerations for food stamp and school lunch programs.
